legacyFallback.mainTextStart: Before Morning6 / 391Before Morning2Morning Commute3Workday4Decision Room5The Day Repeats6Twilight: The Wider Landscape7Midnight Ledger8New DawnOverfitting and GeneralizationMemorizing examples versus learning patternsOverfitting happens when a model learns training examples too narrowly and performs poorly on new examples; generalization means learned patterns work beyond the examples seen during training.Visual aidWhat to picturetoo fittedtrainheld-outoverfitsmoothOverfitting vs Generalization
legacyFallback.question: Which is a sign of overfitting?
legacyFallback.title: Overfitting and Generalization

firstSixQaStates.label: what-is-llm q1 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: What Is an LLM?
firstSixQaStates.question: When an LLM answers a prompt by generating one token at a time, what is the model doing at each step?

firstSixQaStates.label: what-is-llm q1 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: What Is an LLM?
firstSixQaStates.question: When an LLM answers a prompt by generating one token at a time, what is the model doing at each step?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. The model is using fixed learned weights and temporary context to make the next-token step.

firstSixQaStates.label: what-is-llm q2 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: What Is an LLM?
firstSixQaStates.question: An LLM gives a fluent explanation of a poem. What should a model-literate learner conclude?

firstSixQaStates.label: what-is-llm q2 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: What Is an LLM?
firstSixQaStates.question: An LLM gives a fluent explanation of a poem. What should a model-literate learner conclude?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Insight strengthened. This is the fluent-behavior-without-awareness boundary Prompt Life wants learners to hold.

firstSixQaStates.label: where-llms-fit q1 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Where LLMs Fit
firstSixQaStates.question: A campus tool summarizes text with an LLM, while another creates images by denoising a noisy pattern. What does that show?

firstSixQaStates.label: where-llms-fit q1 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Where LLMs Fit
firstSixQaStates.question: A campus tool summarizes text with an LLM, while another creates images by denoising a noisy pattern. What does that show?

firstSixQaStates.label: where-llms-fit q1 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Where LLMs Fit
firstSixQaStates.question: A campus tool summarizes text with an LLM, while another creates images by denoising a noisy pattern. What does that show?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. Generative AI is a family, and different branches can use different mechanisms.

firstSixQaStates.label: where-llms-fit q2 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Where LLMs Fit
firstSixQaStates.question: When people say an LLM sits inside the broader AI family, which category map is most useful?

firstSixQaStates.label: where-llms-fit q2 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Where LLMs Fit
firstSixQaStates.question: When people say an LLM sits inside the broader AI family, which category map is most useful?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Insight strengthened. The map keeps overlapping terms useful without making them interchangeable.

firstSixQaStates.label: where-llms-fit q3 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Where LLMs Fit
firstSixQaStates.question: A chatbot product includes an LLM, a search tool, safety filters, and a user interface. What is the LLM?

firstSixQaStates.label: where-llms-fit q3 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Where LLMs Fit
firstSixQaStates.question: A chatbot product includes an LLM, a search tool, safety filters, and a user interface. What is the LLM?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. A product may wrap an LLM with tools, policies, and interface pieces.

firstSixQaStates.label: history q1 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Rationalists vs Empiricists
firstSixQaStates.question: If a system solves a task only by following hand-written if-then rules, how is it different from an LLM?

firstSixQaStates.label: history q1 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Rationalists vs Empiricists
firstSixQaStates.question: If a system solves a task only by following hand-written if-then rules, how is it different from an LLM?

firstSixQaStates.label: history q1 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Rationalists vs Empiricists
firstSixQaStates.question: If a system solves a task only by following hand-written if-then rules, how is it different from an LLM?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. LLMs mostly belong to the learned-patterns tradition, even when products around them use rules.

firstSixQaStates.label: history q2 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Rationalists vs Empiricists
firstSixQaStates.question: During deep-learning training, why does the system measure loss?

firstSixQaStates.label: history q2 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Rationalists vs Empiricists
firstSixQaStates.question: During deep-learning training, why does the system measure loss?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Insight strengthened. Loss is the numerical training signal, not a feeling or truth meter.

firstSixQaStates.label: history q3 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Rationalists vs Empiricists
firstSixQaStates.question: A university AI app uses an LLM plus policy filters, retrieval, and hand-written rules. What should a model-literate user conclude?

firstSixQaStates.label: history q3 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Rationalists vs Empiricists
firstSixQaStates.question: A university AI app uses an LLM plus policy filters, retrieval, and hand-written rules. What should a model-literate user conclude?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. The product can be hybrid even when the LLM itself is learned from data.

firstSixQaStates.label: training q1 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Training
firstSixQaStates.question: During training, the model predicts a target token and gets it wrong. What makes that moment learning rather than ordinary inference?

firstSixQaStates.label: training q1 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Training
firstSixQaStates.question: During training, the model predicts a target token and gets it wrong. What makes that moment learning rather than ordinary inference?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. The durable weight update is what makes this training.

firstSixQaStates.label: training q2 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Training
firstSixQaStates.question: A student asks a chatbot one question during ordinary use. Which statement is usually true?

firstSixQaStates.label: training q2 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Training
firstSixQaStates.question: A student asks a chatbot one question during ordinary use. Which statement is usually true?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Insight strengthened. Normal chat inference can use context, but it usually does not rewrite weights.

firstSixQaStates.label: training q3 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Training
firstSixQaStates.question: Why can training change how the model behaves tomorrow, while inference usually cannot?

firstSixQaStates.label: training q3 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Training
firstSixQaStates.question: Why can training change how the model behaves tomorrow, while inference usually cannot?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. Durable parameters carry forward; inference-time states usually do not.

firstSixQaStates.label: training q4 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Training
firstSixQaStates.question: In a training loop, which sequence is the durable learning path?

firstSixQaStates.label: training q4 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Training
firstSixQaStates.question: In a training loop, which sequence is the durable learning path?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Insight strengthened. That is the weight-changing path.

firstSixQaStates.label: pretraining q1 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Pretraining
firstSixQaStates.question: During pretraining, what signal teaches the model broad language patterns across many examples?

firstSixQaStates.label: pretraining q1 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Pretraining
firstSixQaStates.question: During pretraining, what signal teaches the model broad language patterns across many examples?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. Pretraining scales the training loop across many examples.

firstSixQaStates.label: pretraining q2 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Pretraining
firstSixQaStates.question: Why can pretraining create broad capability without making the model a perfect library of its sources?

firstSixQaStates.label: pretraining q2 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Pretraining
firstSixQaStates.question: Why can pretraining create broad capability without making the model a perfect library of its sources?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. Pretraining can shape broad ability without turning the model into a perfect source library.

firstSixQaStates.label: pretraining q3 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Pretraining
firstSixQaStates.question: If pretraining examples include many styles and task shapes, what can change inside the model?

firstSixQaStates.label: pretraining q3 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Pretraining
firstSixQaStates.question: If pretraining examples include many styles and task shapes, what can change inside the model?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. Pretraining changes reusable model structure before later prompts arrive.

firstSixQaStates.label: pretraining q4 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Pretraining
firstSixQaStates.question: A base model produces fact-like text about public topics. What is the model-literate explanation?

firstSixQaStates.label: pretraining q4 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Pretraining
firstSixQaStates.question: A base model produces fact-like text about public topics. What is the model-literate explanation?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Insight strengthened. Source-like output can come from pretrained patterns, but it is not the same as cited evidence.

firstSixQaStates.label: overfitting-generalization q1 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Overfitting and Generalization
firstSixQaStates.question: A model gets every training example right but fails on new validation examples. What problem is showing up?

firstSixQaStates.label: overfitting-generalization q1 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Overfitting and Generalization
firstSixQaStates.question: A model gets every training example right but fails on new validation examples. What problem is showing up?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Insight unlocked. Good diagnosis. The held-out examples reveal whether the pattern transfers.

firstSixQaStates.label: overfitting-generalization q2 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Overfitting and Generalization
firstSixQaStates.question: Why do model builders test on held-out validation data instead of only training examples?

firstSixQaStates.label: overfitting-generalization q2 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Overfitting and Generalization
firstSixQaStates.question: Why do model builders test on held-out validation data instead of only training examples?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Insight strengthened. Validation is about transfer, not memorized training performance.

firstSixQaStates.label: overfitting-generalization q3 ready
firstSixQaStates.title: Overfitting and Generalization
firstSixQaStates.question: A model trained heavily on one narrow answer template works on that template but struggles with varied new prompts. What should a model-literate learner suspect?

firstSixQaStates.label: overfitting-generalization q3 correct
firstSixQaStates.title: Overfitting and Generalization
firstSixQaStates.question: A model trained heavily on one narrow answer template works on that template but struggles with varied new prompts. What should a model-literate learner suspect?
firstSixQaStates.feedback: Good distinction. Targeted training can help, but narrow training can also make behavior brittle.
